name: AssemblyAI API
what_it_does: Speech-to-text API with built-in LLM-powered features including transcription, speaker diarization, sentiment analysis, summarization, and PII detection from audio.
use_cases: Transcribing meeting recordings with speaker labels
Building voice-enabled agents that process audio input
Detecting PII in call center audio for compliance
Generating meeting summaries and action items from recordings
Sentiment analysis on customer support calls
not_for: Real-time transcription with sub-100ms latency (use streaming for near-real-time)
Languages with limited audio training data outside their supported set
Very short audio clips where transcription overhead dominates
best_when: You need transcription plus downstream audio intelligence (sentiment, PII, summaries) in one API call.
avoid_when: You only need basic transcription and want the cheapest possible option, or need true real-time streaming with very low latency.

auth.notes: Single API key in Authorization header. No scopes - key has full account access. Use separate keys per environment.

pricing.paid_tiers: ~$0.37/hour for transcription; LLM features (summarization, etc.) add per-feature cost
pricing.notes: Free credits expire. Pricing is per audio hour. Advanced features (sentiment, PII, summarization) are additional per-request charges. Webhook delivery is free.

performance.rate_limits: 5 concurrent transcriptions on free; higher on paid

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Clear error messages with error type and message. Transcription failures include reason codes.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Transcription jobs are create-once; use transcript ID to poll status. Submitting same audio again creates a new job.
known_agent_gotchas: Transcription is async - agents must poll for completion or use webhooks; there is no synchronous mode for long audio
Audio must be publicly accessible via URL or uploaded first via their upload endpoint
LLM features (summarization, entity detection) must be enabled at transcription submission time, not after
Free credits expire after 1 year - agents tested in dev may hit billing surprises in prod
Speaker diarization with many speakers can produce inconsistent speaker labels across similar audio
